file_insights.changes: Created new provider module with CodexCredentials struct, detect() implementation, OAuth token refresh logic, and API fetch for usage metrics
file_insights.significance: Core implementation of Codex OAuth provider

file_insights.changes: Added mod codex declaration and public re-export

patterns_discovered.description: detect() checks: (1) credentials file ~/.codex/auth.json, (2) binary on PATH via which, (3) fallback unavailable. Establishes consistent pattern for provider availability checking.
Tracks last_refresh timestamp in credentials struct; warns if >8 days old before making API calls. Implements defensive refresh check without forcing immediate refresh.
parse_usage_response() maps nested JSON (session.percent_used, weekly.percent_used) to flat struct (primary_pct, secondary_pct) for simple consumption by progress display.
parse_usage_response() tested independently with mock JSON before integration, enabling isolated validation of JSON parsing logic.

gotchas_discovered.description: Hard-coded https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage endpoint is subject to upstream API changes. No versioning or fallback negotiation implemented.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document endpoint version; add monitoring for 404/429 responses

gotchas_discovered.description: 8-day threshold check must account for timezone and system clock drift. Boundary condition edge case: exactly 8 days may trigger warn on one system but not another.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Use >8 days instead of >=8 days; document intent in code comment

gotchas_discovered.description: CodexCredentials contains refresh_token (sensitive). Reading from ~/.codex/auth.json does not validate file permissions (should be 0600).
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add fs::metadata() check post-read; warn if world-readable

gotchas_discovered.description: binary_on_path() uses Command::new("which"), which may not exist on all systems or may be shadowed by shell builtins.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Fallback to PATH iteration if which fails; document POSIX assumption

approach_outcome.summary: Subtask-1-1 completed successfully. CodexProvider scaffold implemented with all five required components: credentials struct, path detection, OAuth fetch, usage parsing, and unit tests. Code compiles and passes basic test cases.
approach_outcome.scope_adherence: All acceptance criteria met: detect() checks both auth.json and PATH, OAuth refresh logic with 8-day warn threshold, parse_usage_response() maps nested JSON correctly, unit tests verify parser with mock JSON.
approach_outcome.integration_status: Provider module integrated into crates/bop-cli via mod.rs; ready for adapter and main CLI integration in subsequent subtasks

recommendations.item: Add file permission check (0600) when reading ~/.codex/auth.json to prevent accidental credential leakage
Implement exponential backoff + retry loop for OAuth API fetch; handle 429 (rate limit) and 503 (service unavailable) gracefully
Document OAuth endpoint version in code; add comment explaining 8-day refresh threshold rationale and any upstream SLA assumptions
Add integration test: mock HTTP server returning malformed JSON, missing fields, and network timeouts to verify error handling
Replace which-command dependency with PATH iteration fallback to improve Windows compatibility and remove external tool dependency
Add debug logging for OAuth refresh lifecycle: timestamp comparisons, API latency, usage metric extraction. Use log crate with structured fields.
